# Add people to a channel

**Who can use this feature?**

- All **members** and **Multi-Channel Guests**
- Available on **[all plans](https://slack.com/pricing)**

Slack organizes conversations into dedicated spaces called [channels](https://slack.com/help/articles/360017938993). Add people to channels to bring the right people and information in your workspace together in one place (for people outside your company, learn how to [use Slack Connect to invite them to a channel](https://slack.com/help/articles/360035092414-Use-Slack-Connect-to-work-with-other-companies-in-channels)).

![GIF showing a cursor adding people to a channel in Slack](https://slack.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32820041153171)

## Add people to a channel

You can add up to 1,000 people to a channel at once. For large groups, we recommend [copying a list of full names or email addresses from another channel in Slack](https://slack.com/help/articles/4409607962387-Copy-member-names-and-email-addresses-from-a-conversation) or another service before following the steps below. If [email addresses are hidden](https://slack.com/help/articles/228020667-Manage-email-display) in member profiles, you won't be able to add people to channels using email addresses.

**Tip:** To help onboard new members, owners and admins can [set default channels](https://slack.com/help/articles/201898998-Set-default-channels-for-new-members) for them to automatically join.

DesktopMobile

1. Open the channel you'd like to add people to.
2. Click cluster of profile pictures in the top right.
3. Click **Add people**.
4. To add a few people, search for the members you'd like to add by name, email address, or [user group](https://slack.com/help/articles/212906697-Create-and-edit-user-groups) (if you're using a paid plan) and select them. To add a large group, paste the list of names or email addresses into the field.
5. When you're done, click **Add**.

1. Open the channel you’d like to add people to.
2. Tap the channel name at the top.
3. Tap **Add**.
4. To add a few people, search for the members you'd like to add by name or email address and select them. To add a large group, paste the list of names or email addresses into the field.
5. When you’re done, tap **Add**.

**Tip:** On paid plans, you can invite a usergroup to a channel on mobile with the /invite @\[usergroup\] [slash command](https://slack.com/help/articles/201259356).

**Note:** On the Business+ and Enterprise plans, owners and admins with permission can add people to channels with [channel management tools](https://slack.com/help/articles/360047512554-Use-channel-management-tools).

# Invite new members to your workspace

**Who can use this feature?**

- **Workspace Owners**, **Workspace Admins**, and **members**(by default)
- Available on [**all plans**](https://slack.com/pricing)

By default, members (but not guests) can invite new members to their workspace, but owners and admins can change this so invitations [require admin approval](https://slack.com/help/articles/115004854783-Require-admin-approval-for-invitations-to-Slack#decide-who-can-send-invitations). If admin approval is required, members can request invitations for admins to approve or deny.

**Tip:** Looking to add someone to a channel in your workspace? Visit [Add people to a channel](https://slack.com/help/articles/201980108-Add-people-to-a-channel) to learn how.

## Send an invitation

The ability to send invitations depends on your [role in Slack](https://slack.com/help/articles/360018112273-Types-of-roles-in-Slack), as well as your workspace settings and plan. An invitation will be active for 30 days after you send it. Owners and admins can [resend a pending invitation](https://slack.com/help/articles/360060363633-Manage-pending-invitations-and-invite-links-for-your-workspace#manage-pending-invitations) to reactivate it.

### Free version of Slack

On the free version of Slack, owners, admins, and members can invite new members to their workspace.

DesktopiOSAndroid

1. Click your workspace name in the sidebar.

![](https://a.slack-edge.com/8e8e4/helpcenter/img/workspace-picker@2x.jpg)
2. Select **Invite people to...** from the menu.
3. Enter the email address for anyone you'd like to invite.
4. Click **Send**, then click **Done**.

1. From the **DMs tab**, tap the **plus sign**.
2. Tap **Invite**.
3. Enter the email address for anyone you'd like to invite, or tap **Invite from** **Contacts** to select people saved to your device.
4. Tap **Send**.

1. From the **DMs tab**, tap the **plus sign**.
2. Tap **Invite**.
3. Enter the email address for anyone you'd like to invite, or tap **Contacts** to invite people saved to your device.
4. Tap **Send**.

**Note:** Workspace Owners can [approve certain email domains](https://slack.com/help/articles/115004856503-Manage-how-people-join-your-workspace) so that anyone with an approved email address can join their workspace.

### Pro, Business+, and Enterprise plans

On paid plans, owners, admins, and members can invite new members to their workspace. Workspace Owners and Workspace Admins can also invite [guests](https://slack.com/help/articles/202518103-Understand-guest-roles-in-Slack).

DesktopiOSAndroid

1. Click your workspace name in the sidebar.

![](https://a.slack-edge.com/8e8e4/helpcenter/img/workspace-picker@2x.jpg)
2. Select **Invite people to...** from the menu.
3. Enter the email address for anyone you'd like to invite. If you're a Workspace Owner or Admin, click the drop-down menu to choose whether you'd like to invite **members** or **guests**.
4. If you're inviting **members**, click **Customize your invitation** to select additional [default channels](https://slack.com/help/articles/201898998-Set-default-channels-for-new-members) they'll be added to if they accept their invitations. If you're inviting **guests**, choose the channel(s) they'll be added to and set a time limit for their accounts if you'd like.
5. Click **Send**, then click **Done**.

**Note:** It's not currently possible to invite guests from Slack's mobile apps.

1. From the **DMs tab**, tap the **plus sign**.
2. Tap **Invite**.
3. Enter the email address for anyone you'd like to invite, or tap **Invite from** **Contacts** to select people saved to your device.
4. Tap **Send**.

**Note:** It's not currently possible to invite guests from Slack's mobile apps.

1. From the **DMs tab**, tap the **plus sign**.
2. Tap **Invite**.
3. Enter email address for anyone you'd like to invite, or tap **Contacts** to invite people saved to your device.
4. Tap **Send**.

**Note:** On the Enterprise Grid plan, Org Owners can prevent their members from joining external workspaces using an email address on a [restricted company domain](https://slack.com/help/articles/5513043606547-Claim-and-verify-email-domains#enterprise-grid-plan-1).

## Share an invitation link

On the free, Pro, and Business+ plans, members with permission to send invitations can also share an invite link. Each invite link is active for 30 days, and can be used by up to 400 people. If your invite link expires, [find an owner or admin](https://slack.com/help/articles/360003534892-Browse-people-and-user-groups-in-Slack#find-owners-and-admins) who can [manage invite links](https://slack.com/help/articles/360060363633-Manage-pending-invitations-and-invite-links-for-your-workspace#manage-invitation-links) in Slack.

Keep in mind, anyone who clicks the link will be able to join your workspace. To safeguard the privacy and integrity of your workspace and our platform, be sure to share the link securely, and only with those who should have access to the contents of your workspace.

DesktopiOSAndroid

1. Click your workspace name in the sidebar.

![](https://a.slack-edge.com/8e8e4/helpcenter/img/workspace-picker@2x.jpg)
2. Select **Invite people to...** from the menu.
3. Click **Copy invite link**.
4. If you’d like, click **Edit link settings** to choose an expiration date and decide if you’ll receive notifications from Slackbot when someone uses your link.

1. From the **Home** tab, swipe right.
2. Tap the **three dots icon** next to your workspace name.
3. Tap **Invite Members**.
4. Tap **Share a link**, then send the link to anyone you'd like to invite.

It's not currently possible to create invite links on the Android app.

**Note:** You won't see the option to create an invite link if [single sign-on (SSO)](https://slack.com/help/articles/220403548-Manage-single-sign-on-settings) has been enabled for your workspace. If you don't see this option, [find an owner or admin](https://slack.com/help/articles/360003534892-Browse-people-and-user-groups-in-Slack) to ask for help.

## Request an invitation

If members don’t have permission to send invitations, they can request them instead. Members will get a message from Slackbot when an admin has approved or denied their invitation request.

DesktopiOSAndroid

1. Click your workspace name in the sidebar.

![](https://a.slack-edge.com/8e8e4/helpcenter/img/workspace-picker@2x.jpg)
2. Select **Invite people to...** from the menu.
3. Enter the email address for anyone you'd like to invite. If you're on the Pro, Business+, or Enterprise Grid plan, click the drop-down menu to request invitations for **members** or **guests**.
4. If you're inviting **members**, click **Customize your invitation** to select additional [default channels](https://slack.com/help/articles/201898998-Set-default-channels-for-new-members) they'll be added to if they accept their invitations. If you're inviting **guests**, choose the channel(s) they'll be added to and set a time limit for their accounts if you'd like.
5. Click **Send Request**.

1. From the **Home** tab, swipe right.
2. Tap the **three dots icon** next to your workspace name.
3. Tap **Invite Members**.
4. Enter the email address of the person you'd like to invite. You'll see the option to enter additional emails once you start typing.
5. Tap **Send Request**.

1. From the **Home** tab, swipe right.
2. Tap the **three dots icon** next to your workspace name.
3. Tap **Invite Members**.
4. Enter the email address of the person you'd like to invite. You'll see the option to enter additional emails once you start typing.
5. Tap the **check mark**.
6. Tap **Send Request**.

## Invitation limits

In certain circumstances, Slack may limit your ability to send invitations. This typically happens if you’ve sent many invites, but few have been accepted. To avoid hitting an invite limit, you can [share an invite link](https://slack.com/help/articles/201330256-Invite-new-members-to-your-workspace#h_01FGMBMJ8XZAXG4S1G94KJZYST) instead.

If you hit an invite limit, all of your pending invitations will remain valid. Please note that revoking pending invites will not lift an invitation limit.

# Use Slack Connect to work with other companies in channels

**Who can use this feature?**

- All **members**(by default)
- Available on [**paid plans**](https://slack.com/pricing?utm_source=helpcenter&utm_medium=hyperlink&utm_content=rolesdiv)

Rather than using email to communicate, you can use [Slack Connect](https://slack.com/connect) to work alongside people from other companies in the same Slack channel. When you work with other companies in Slack, people can only access the channels they’ve been invited to join.

**Note:** Admins can restrict who can invite external people to channels. If you don’t see this option, [find an owner or admin](https://slack.com/help/articles/360003534892-Browse-people-and-user-groups-in-Slack) and ask for help.

## Add people from another company to a channel

Send the people you’d like to work with an invitation to join your channel (each Slack Connect channel can host up to 250 organizations, including your own). Keep in mind that you cannot add external people to the [#general or #all-companyname channel](https://slack.com/help/articles/220105027-Use-the-general-channel-to-share-announcements).

DesktopiOSAndroid

1. Open the channel you'd like to invite people to. (You can also [create a new channel](https://slack.com/help/articles/201402297-Create-a-channel).)
2. Click the channel name in the conversation header.

![Gif of a cursor clicking the channel name in the channel header](https://slack.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32818174155539)
3. Select the **Settings** tab.
4. Under **Slack Connect**, click **Add people**.
5. Enter the email address or name for anyone you’d like to invite to the channel, then click **Add**.
6. Select **An external organization**, then click **Next**.
7. If you have [permission](https://slack.com/help/articles/360050528953-Manage-who-can-join-Slack-Connect-channels-owned-by-other-organizations#inviting-people-to-channels), you can choose if people [**Can post & invite** or **Can only post**](https://slack.com/help/articles/1500012572621-Manage-Slack-Connect-channel-invitation-settings-and-permissions) to the channel you're inviting them to. Select **Next** to continue.
8. Add an optional note if you'd like, then click **Send Invitation**.

1. Open the channel you'd like to invite people to. (You can also [create a new channel](https://slack.com/help/articles/201402297-Create-a-channel).)
2. Tap the channel name at the top.
3. Tap **Add**.
4. Enter the email address or name for anyone you’d like to invite to the channel, then tap **Add**.
5. Select **An external organization**, then tap **Next**.
6. If you have [permission](https://slack.com/help/articles/360050528953-Manage-who-can-join-Slack-Connect-channels-owned-by-other-organizations#inviting-people-to-channels), you can choose if people [**Can post & invite** or **Can only post**](https://slack.com/help/articles/1500012572621-Manage-Slack-Connect-channel-invitation-settings-and-permissions) to the channel you're inviting them to. Tap **Next** to continue.
7. Add an optional note if you'd like, then tap **Send invitation**.

1. Open the channel you'd like to invite people to. (You can also [create a new channel](https://slack.com/help/articles/201402297-Create-a-channel).)
2. Tap the channel name at the top.
3. Tap **Add**.
4. Enter the email address or name for anyone you’d like to invite to the channel, then tap **Add**.
5. Select **An external organization**, then tap **Next**.
6. If you have [permission](https://slack.com/help/articles/360050528953-Manage-who-can-join-Slack-Connect-channels-owned-by-other-organizations#inviting-people-to-channels), you can choose if people [**Can post & invite** or **Can only post**](https://slack.com/help/articles/1500012572621-Manage-Slack-Connect-channel-invitation-settings-and-permissions) to the channel you're inviting them to. Tap **Next** to continue.
7. Add an optional note if you'd like, then tap **Send invitation**.

**Note:** Your admins may need to approve invitations you send. Depending on each company’s Slack settings, people may also need admin approval to accept your invitation.

## Revoke an invitation to a channel

You can revoke an invitation you’ve sent if it hasn’t been accepted yet.

DesktopiOSAndroid

1. From **Home**, click **Directories**.
2. Click **External** at the top of the page.
3. Click **View invitations**.
4. Hover over the invitation you’d like to revoke and click the **revoke icon**.
5. Select **Revoke** to confirm.

1. Tap your profile photo in the top right.
2. Tap **Invitations to connect**.
3. Tap the **Sent** tab.
4. Locate the invitation, then tap **Revoke.**

1. Tap your profile photo in the top right.
2. Tap **Slack Connect invitations**.
3. Tap the **Sent** tab.
4. Locate the invitation, then tap **Revoke**.

**Note:** If you don't see **Directories**, look for the **External connections** tab in the sidebar instead. You may need to click **More** to find it.

**Tip:** People with permission to manage Slack Connect channels can [revoke any pending invitation](https://slack.com/help/articles/115005912706-Manage-Slack-Connect-channel-approval-settings-and-invitation-requests#revoke-invitations) sent by someone from their organization.

## Adjust existing permissions

Once the invitation has been accepted, anyone with permission to manage [Slack Connect approval settings](https://slack.com/help/articles/115005912706-Manage-Slack-Connect-channel-approval-settings-and-invitation-requests#manage-approval-settings-for-channels) can adjust the permissions for external people.

**Note:** Each member’s ability to upgrade or downgrade permissions will depend on the [type of invitation](https://slack.com/help/articles/1500012572621-Manage-Slack-Connect-channel-invitation-settings-and-permissions) they’re allowed to send.

DesktopiOSAndroid

1. Open the channel you’d like to manage permissions for.
2. Click the channel name in the conversation header.

![Gif of a cursor clicking the channel name in the channel header](https://slack.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32818174155539)
3. In the **Organizations** section, click the **three dots icon** next to the team you'd like to upgrade or downgrade.
4. Click **Manage permissions.**
5. Choose if the team **Can post & invite** or **Can only post**, then click **Next.**
6. Click **Save**.

1. Open the channel you’d like to manage permissions for.
2. Tap the channel name in the channel header.
3. Tap **Organizations**, then select the team you'd like to upgrade or downgrade.
4. Tap **Manage permissions.**
5. Tap the toggle next to **Let them invite their teammates** on or off.

1. Open the channel you’d like to manage permissions for.
2. Tap the channel name in the channel header.
3. Tap **Organizations**, then select the team you'd like to upgrade or downgrade.
4. Tap **Manage permissions.**
5. Tap the toggle next to **Let them invite their teammates** on or off.

**Tip:** On the Business+ and Enterprise plans, those with permission to send invitations who also have [channel management permissions](https://slack.com/help/articles/360052445454-Manage-permissions-for-channel-management-tools) can use the [channel management tools](https://slack.com/help/articles/360047512554-Use-channel-management-tools#view-and-manage-channels) to upgrade or downgrade external people.

# Use Salesforce channels in Slack

**Who can use this feature?**

- All **members**
- Available on [**all plans**](https://slack.com/pricing)

Salesforce channels are a type of Slack [channel](https://slack.com/help/articles/360017938993-What-is-a-channel) that correspond to Salesforce records, like Accounts or Opportunities. Salesforce channels let your team centralize conversations about customers, drive deal progress, and update Salesforce records, all in Slack.

**Note:** Owners and admins must [configure Salesforce channels](https://slack.com/help/articles/32665140498195-Set-up-and-manage-Salesforce-channels-in-Slack) in order for members to use them. If you’re not able to use Salesforce channels in Slack, [reach out to an owner or admin](https://slack.com/help/articles/360003534892-Browse-people-and-user-groups-in-Slack#find-owners-and-admins) and ask for help.

### ![GIF demonstrating Salesforce channels in Slack](https://slack.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33315911958675)  How it works

- Salesforce channel names match the names of their corresponding records, which means they can include spaces, capital letters, and other characters that are not allowed in standard Slack channels. You can't [rename](https://slack.com/help/articles/201654073-Rename-a-channel) Salesforce channels in Slack.
- When you create or join a Salesforce channel, it’ll be added to your sidebar in a **Salesforce channels** section (but you can move it to another [custom section](https://slack.com/help/articles/360043207674-Organize-your-sidebar-with-custom-sections) if you’d like).
- Your permissions in Salesforce channels match your permissions for the corresponding record. If you have permission to access the record in Salesforce, you can access the corresponding Salesforce channel; if you have permission to edit the record in Salesforce, you can edit the record in Slack.

**Note:** It’s not possible to invite [external people](https://slack.com/help/articles/360035092414-Use-Slack-Connect-to-work-with-other-companies-in-channels) to Salesforce channels.

## Components of a Salesforce channel

The features of your Salesforce channels are organized into tabs. These are the tabs you can expect to see in a Salesforce channel and what they can help you do.

**Messages**

Use the messages tab for conversations related to the corresponding record like updating your teammates on an important deal or discussing a new Opportunity. Just like a typical Slack channel, these conversations can include discussions with channel members, messages from apps, and more.

**Record details**

The record details tab uses data from Salesforce to give you a snapshot of the record the channel is mapped to. From the record details tab, you can learn key details about the record and its related accounts and [update it](https://slack.com/help/articles/32664824986259-Use-Salesforce-channels-in-Slack#h_01J74403EAPZ782K9DDPNGDV8F) right in Slack. The name of the record details tab will match the type of record the Salesforce channel is associated with. For example, a Salesforce channel associated with an Account will have an **Account details** tab.

**Summary**(available on the **Business+** and **Enterprise+** plans)

Use the summary tab to get up to speed quickly with an [AI-generated](https://slack.com/help/articles/25076892548883-Guide-to-AI-features-in-Slack) summary of conversations in all of the Salesforce channels associated with the corresponding record. Click the **More details icon** next to a summary to review its sources and jump to the conversation in Slack.

**Related conversations**

Find other relevant conversations connected by Salesforce record relationship in the related conversations tab. When you select the related conversations tab, you’ll see other channels that are automatically associated with the Salesforce channel based on record relationships, but you can also click **Add** to manually add a channel to the list of related channels.

**Related records**

If your admins choose a record type for the **Default list tab** when configuring Salesforce channels, that record type will display in the related records tab. If you have permission to update the record type, any changes you make in Slack will be reflected in Salesforce.

**Note:** Any channel member can participate in the **Messages** tab, but access to Salesforce data is based on a user's permissions to view and edit Salesforce records.

## Create a Salesforce channel

You can create a new Salesforce channel from any record that appears in Slack. On the Enterprise Grid plan, only people who can [create org-wide multi-workspace channels](https://slack.com/help/articles/115001399587-Add-a-channel-to-multiple-workspaces-in-your-Enterprise-organization#create-a-multi-workspace-channel) can convert a channel to a Salesforce channel.

DesktopMobile

1. From a record in Slack, click **Create Channel** in the top-right corner.
2. Choose to **Create a new channel** or **Link an existing channel**, then follow the prompts to finish.

1. From a record in Slack, tap **Create Channel**.
2. Tap **Create Channel** or **Link Existing channel** then follow the prompts to finish

**Note:** If a Salesforce channel has already been created for a record’s [parent record](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000391142&type=1), you’ll be prompted to use that channel (but you can still create a new one if you’d like).

## Update a record from a Salesforce channel

To save time switching between Slack and Salesforce, you can update a record right from the channel. You can edit a record in Slack if you have permission to edit it in Salesforce. It's not possible to edit rich text and geolocation [Salesforce fields](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.custom_field_types.htm&type=5) in Slack.

DesktopMobile

1. Open the Salesforce channel for the record you'd like to update.
2. Select the **Record details** tab.
3. Under **Record details**, update any fields you have permission to edit.

1. Open the Salesforce channel for the record you'd like to update.
2. Tap the conversation header then select **Opportunity details**.
3. Next to **Record details** tap **Edit**, then update any fields you have permission to edit.

**Note:** On Business+ and Enterprise plans, use [list views](https://slack.com/help/articles/45520056210579-View-and-update-Salesforce-data-in-Slack#access-list-views) to share customized collections of records and update Salesforce data in Slack.

## Automate Salesforce channel invitations

On the Business+ and Enterprise Grid plans, use [Workflow Builder](https://slack.com/help/articles/360035692513-Guide-to-Slack-Workflow-Builder) to automatically add relevant stakeholders to a Salesforce channel when an event is triggered in Salesforce. For example, you can set up a workflow to create a Salesforce channel and add the Opportunity Owner and Sales Manager when the opportunity amount decreases by 10%.

1. From your desktop, click **Tools** in the sidebar.
2. From **Workflows**, click **New** in the top right.
3. Select **Build Workflow**.
4. Below **Start the workflow**, click **Choose an event**, then select **Start with a Salesforce event**.
5. Set up your workflow trigger, then click **Continue**.
6. Click **Add steps**, then select **Salesforce channels**.
7. Choose **Create Salesforce channel** or **Find Salesforce channel from record ID**, then configure the step.
8. Below **Suggested step**, select **Add people to a channel**, then configure the step.
9. Complete your workflow, then select **Finish up**.

**Note:** If you don't see **Tools**, look for **Automations** in the sidebar instead. You may need to click **More**.

## Use Salesforce channels in Salesforce

If [configured by your Salesforce System Admins](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=slack.slack_salesforce_setup.htm), you can also [access a Salesforce channel](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=slack.slack_salesforce_channels_use.htm) from its corresponding record directly in Salesforce. Any messages and updates will sync instantly across both Slack and Salesforce, so you can communicate with your team where it's most convenient for you.

### What you can do

Here are the actions you can take from Salesforce channels in Salesforce:

- Send and format messages
- Reply in threads
- Upload files
- Add emojis and use emoji reactions
- Mention people
- Invite people to channels

**Note:** The Slack experience in Salesforce is limited. More complex actions such [huddles](https://slack.com/help/articles/4402059015315-Use-huddles-in-Slack), [clips](https://slack.com/help/articles/4406235165587-Record-audio-and-video-clips-in-Slack), and [workflows](https://slack.com/help/articles/360035692513-Guide-to-Slack-Workflow-Builder) can only be completed in Slack. For more information, visit the [Salesforce Help Center](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=slack.slack_salesforce_channels_communicate.htm&type=5).
